item.title: Silent .catch(() => {}) in sanitizer.ts swallows audit log and quarantine write errors
item.intent: No security event in the sanitizer module is silently discarded. Both audit log write failures and quarantine write failures must log a warning. A sanitizer that silently fails to log threats is indistinguishable from one that has no threats.
item.description: src/secure/sanitizer/sanitizer.ts lines 125 and 130 use fire-and-forget .catch(() => {}) on writeAuditLog() and writeQuarantine() calls. This silently swallows errors from audit log and quarantine file writes with zero observability. If writes fail (disk full, permissions, path errors), the sanitizer continues as if nothing happened — threat events are never logged and injected content is never quarantined, with no trace. The audit logger (BUG-015) sweep fixed silent catches in audit/logger.ts, but missed these two callers in sanitizer.ts. Pattern: identical to BUG-012 through BUG-019 (add console.warn, keep fallback behavior). Steps: (1) open src/secure/sanitizer/sanitizer.ts; (2) replace .catch(() => {}) on line 125 with .catch(err => console.warn('[sanitizer] audit log write failed:', err)); (3) replace .catch(() => {}) on line 130 with .catch(err => console.warn('[sanitizer] quarantine write failed:', err)); (4) run secure/ tests. Scope: 2 one-line changes in one file. Zero behavior change — fire-and-forget is correct, just needs to log.
